documentation: Anchor truth file for the S2 EDGAR-ingest anchor-validation gate. Values are fiscal-2023 figures from each company's 10-K, verified against the SEC company-facts API on 2026-06-06. fiscal_year_end is the date EDGAR uses for the FY 2023 frame. Notable findings from Step 1 verification: (a) AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, and JNJ all tag revenue under RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax and emit NO FY2023 Revenues fact, exercising the concept-map fallback path. (b) JPM lands in Case A: emits Revenues=158,104,000,000 (matches published total net revenue), so the top-priority resolver works for the bank without concept_map.py modification. (c) JNJ NetIncomeLoss=35,153,000,000 correctly includes the Kenvue separation gain.

tolerance.comment: 0.5% tolerance allows for tiny rounding differences in our unit-scaling path; concept-map mis-resolution will overshoot this by orders of magnitude (e.g. 1e3 or 1e9), surfacing the bug clearly.

anchors.source: AAPL 10-K filed 2023-11-03; SEC company-facts CIK0000320193. Modern Apple tags revenue via the contract-revenue concept and emits NO FY2023 Revenues fact; this anchor exercises the concept-map fallback path.
MSFT 10-K filed 2023-07-27; SEC company-facts CIK0000789019. MSFT, like AAPL, emits no FY2023 Revenues fact and resolves via the contract-revenue fallback concept.
GOOGL 10-K filed 2024-01-31; SEC company-facts CIK0001652044. GOOGL also emits no FY2023 Revenues fact; revenue resolves via the contract-revenue fallback concept.

JNJ 10-K filed 2024-02-16; SEC company-facts CIK0000200406. JNJ emits no FY2023 Revenues fact; revenue resolves via the contract-revenue fallback. NetIncomeLoss=35,153,000,000 correctly includes the Kenvue separation gain (~$20.9B); the continuing-ops-only ~14B figure is NOT what NetIncomeLoss returns.

JPM 10-K filed 2024-02-16; SEC company-facts CIK0000019617. Case A: JPM emits a Revenues XBRL fact equal to 158,104,000,000 (matches the published 'total net revenue' figure exactly). The top-priority resolver works for the bank without concept_map.py modification; JPM also emits RevenuesNetOfInterestExpense with the same value as a redundant tag.

comparative_anchor_hints.comment: Used by tests/data/test_fixture_integrity.py to guard against fiscal_year-from-fy mislabeling (Critical 2). A FY2022 comparative in the FY2023 10-K carries fy=2023; if normalize derives fiscal_year from fy instead of end.year, the 2022 value lands as a FY2023 row and this fixture-integrity test catches it.
comparative_anchor_hints.source: AAPL FY2022 10-K filed 2022-10-28; comparative in the FY2023 10-K carries fy=2023 with end=2022-09-24.

additional_fixture_tickers.role: mid-cap healthcare (Zoetis); has 10+ year filing history since 2013 spinoff
short-history ticker (Snowflake, IPO 2020); exercises TiRex fallback chain in S7
